channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Musk continued his testimony from yesterday in lawsuit against OpenAI
item.description: Elon Musk takes the stand again Wednesday as a witness in his suit against ChatGPT maker OpenAI.
item.content:encoded: Elon Musk takes the stand again Wednesday as a witness in his suit against ChatGPT maker OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: Rachael Myrow

item.title: Trump administration moves to dismiss members of the National Science Board
item.description: The Trump administration has moved to dismiss the members of the National Science Board, which governs the National Science Foundation. Dismissed members say the agency's independence is threatened.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration has moved to dismiss the members of the National Science Board, which governs the National Science Foundation. Dismissed members say the agency's independence is threatened.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: Camp Mystic parents ask Texas lawmakers to block the camp from re-opening
item.description: A Texas legislative commission heard testimony from families of some of the people who died in the 2025 flooding. Owners of the all-girls Camp Mystic also testified about emergency preparedness plans.
item.content:encoded: A Texas legislative commission heard testimony from families of some of the people who died in the 2025 flooding. Owners of the all-girls Camp Mystic also testified about emergency preparedness plans.
item.dc:creator: Kailey Hunt

item.title: Supreme Court appears to lean toward ending TPS for some migrants
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court appeared sympathetic to the Trump administration's move to end temporary protected status for Haitians and Syrians in the country.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. Supreme Court appeared sympathetic to the Trump administration's move to end temporary protected status for Haitians and Syrians in the country.



(Image credit: Roberto Schmidt)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Three months into the Iran war, how does Beijing assess the conflict?
item.description: As the Iran war enters its third month, we ask how China is making sense of the conflict.
item.content:encoded: As the Iran war enters its third month, we ask how China is making sense of the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Pak

item.title: World Cup cities slow to reveal FIFA required human rights protection plans
item.description: FIFA is requiring World Cup host cities to have a human rights action plan in place before the games begin. So far, only a handful of the 16 host cities have made their plans public.
item.content:encoded: FIFA is requiring World Cup host cities to have a human rights action plan in place before the games begin. So far, only a handful of the 16 host cities have made their plans public.
item.dc:creator: Marlon Hyde

item.title: Kids in Louisville get their own Kentucky Derby parade
item.description: The Kentucky Derby is celebrated by locals in Louisville — including its smallest residents, who get their own downtown parade.
item.content:encoded: The Kentucky Derby is celebrated by locals in Louisville — including its smallest residents, who get their own downtown parade.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Feldhaus Adams

item.title: A look at one of the farms RFK Jr. has pointed to as a model for 'reparenting'
item.description: The U.S. health secretary says he wants to shift addiction care toward an approach that includes rural farms or camps for people in recovery. Many addiction experts say the idea is outdated.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. health secretary says he wants to shift addiction care toward an approach that includes rural farms or camps for people in recovery. Many addiction experts say the idea is outdated.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Belle Burden's marriage ended, and she found her voice
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with author Belle Burden about her memoir Strangers, and the powerful response the book is having.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with author Belle Burden about her memoir Strangers, and the powerful response the book is having.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Dorning

item.title: Dirty nickel: The health costs of mining in Indonesia
item.description: Indonesia is in the midst of a nickel boom, but there are concerns about how the mineral is mined and refined, and about its cost to the environment and local health.
item.content:encoded: Indonesia is in the midst of a nickel boom, but there are concerns about how the mineral is mined and refined, and about its cost to the environment and local health.
item.dc:creator: Katerina Barton
